refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 second).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model
Batch update mechanism to replace the entire worksite model at once
Event-driven update trigger based on detected changes in the environment
Scheduled periodic update trigger at fixed intervals
Parallel processing of update data streams to reduce latency
Prioritization and filtering of update data to focus on critical changes only
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental update mechanism to apply only changes to the worksite model

potential_sources: Tight coupling between update trigger and data processing modules
Shared state dependencies between update mechanism and model storage
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP supports independence by focusing on incremental updates, which can be modularized separately from the triggering and storage components. Care must be taken to maintain clear interfaces to avoid coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that incremental changes can be detected and isolated efficiently
Risk that incremental updates may become complex if changes are interdependent
Assumes that the update latency target (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) is achievable with incremental updates under expected workloads
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining interfaces between update detection, incremental update application, and model storage to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing and updating the worksite model, including applying incremental updates to reflect changes efficiently.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and status data from Vehicle and sends updated model state.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and update instructions related to worksite changes.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles communication with external systems and components for synchronization of worksite model updates.

module_containment_scores.notes: Highly cohesive as the incremental update mechanism logically belongs here; minimal diffusion as updates are internal to the solution logic.
Low containment since Vehicle focuses on physical actuation and sensing; incremental update logic would be scattered or duplicated.
Partial containment possible but teleoperation focuses on remote control rather than model management; would cause diffusion.
Poor containment; safety services focus on alerts and safety monitoring, unrelated to model update logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which is responsible for managing the worksite model and its state. This keeps the update logic centralized and cohesive, minimizing diffusion across unrelated modules.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if updates require frequent real-time data exchange, risking some diffusion.
If the NGHE Solution module becomes too large or complex, it may hinder maintainability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision needed; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and minimal diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP of an incremental update mechanism directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on applying only changes, which supports efficient and timely updates.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules if updates require frequent real-time data exchange, risking some diffusion.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The incremental update mechanism is highly cohesive within the NGHE Solution module, with a high containment score (9) and low diffusion score (2). This indicates good architectural packaging and minimal scattering of the DP across multiple IMs.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the worksite model update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) and ensuring compliance with operational policies and safety plans, which can be traced to the NGHE Solution module implementation.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate is clearly the NGHE Solution module, which encompasses the core logic for managing and updating the worksite model, providing clear allocation of responsibility.

recommended_revisions: No revision needed; the current DP aligns well with the NGHE Solution module boundary, ensuring good containment and minimal diffusion.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification, indicating that the current functional requirement and its design and implementation mappings are well-defined, feasible, and architecturally sound. The incremental update mechanism DP effectively addresses the FR with good containment and minimal diffusion within the NGHE Solution module. Verification is feasible and ownership is clear. Given the high safety criticality, it is prudent to maintain the current level without further decomposition to avoid unnecessary complexity and maintain traceability and control.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the timeliness and safety requirements for updating the worksite model.
The NGHE Solution module is capable of containing the DP with minimal diffusion and coupling risks.
Verification methods for latency and compliance are reliable and traceable.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may pose diffusion risks if real-time data exchange increases beyond current expectations.
evidence_needed: Performance data confirming update latency p95 ≤ 1.0 s under operational conditions.
Verification reports demonstrating compliance with operational policies and safety plans.
Architectural analysis confirming containment and diffusion scores remain stable as system evolves.
